title: Conventions used in the BIS Quarterly Review
summary: This source is a data dictionary page from the BIS Quarterly Review explaining notation conventions (abbreviations for scales, units, missing values), currency codes, and country codes used in BIS publications. It contains no market-relevant analysis, policy discussion, or macro catalyst information.
key_points: Contains notation conventions: lhs (left-hand scale), rhs (right-hand scale), billion/thousand/million abbreviations, and symbols for missing data (. = not available, – = not applicable, nil)
Lists currency codes for approximately 50 currencies including major ones: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, CNY, etc.
Lists country/territory codes organized alphabetically by two-letter ISO code
Explains that 'country' terminology includes territorial entities that are not states as understood by international law
Provides no economic data, policy analysis, or market commentary
why_it_matters: This source has no relevance to market structure or macro catalysts. It is purely a technical reference page documenting formatting standards for BIS statistical publications. It contains no actionable information for financial research, trading, or investment analysis.

human_review.notes: Source is a technical data dictionary with no relevance to finance research or macro analysis. Should be excluded from archive.

notes: IRRELEVANT: This BIS conventions page documents data formatting standards (notation, currency codes, country codes) only. Contains zero content related to market structure, macro catalysts, monetary policy, economic data, or any other investment-relevant topic. Properly rejected as non-archivable for finance research purposes.
